name: Stress test — adversarial queries, ambiguous routing, maximum paper count

setup.description: Final hardening cycle. 4 papers with mixed drug classes and outcomes. Tests adversarial query that tries to trick the system into fabricating data, and an ambiguous query that could be routed as either audio or study guide. Validates that the system handles the largest paper set correctly and produces accurate, grounded responses under stress.

papers.title: TOPCAT: Treatment of Preserved Cardiac Function Heart Failure with an Aldosterone Antagonist
DAPA-HF: Dapagliflozin in Patients with Heart Failure and Reduced Ejection Fraction
DELIVER: Dapagliflozin in Heart Failure with Mildly Reduced or Preserved Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Spironolactone reduced mortality with a relative risk of death of 0.70 (95% CI 0.60-0.82; P<0.001). The trial was stopped early due to clear survival benefit after a mean follow-up of 24 months.
1663 patients with severe heart failure (NYHA class III or IV) and LVEF ≤35% were randomized to spironolactone 25mg or placebo.
Spironolactone did NOT significantly reduce the primary composite endpoint of cardiovascular death, aborted cardiac arrest, or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.89; 95% CI 0.77-1.04; P=0.14).
3445 patients aged ≥50 years with symptomatic heart failure and ejection fraction ≥45% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 3.3 years.
Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.74; 95% CI 0.65-0.85; P<0.001). The number needed to treat was 21 over a median follow-up of 18.2 months.
Enrolled patients were aged ≥18 years with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF ≤40%. Patients were randomized 1:1 to dapagliflozin 10mg or placebo.

Dapagliflozin reduced the composite of worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death (HR 0.82; 95% CI 0.73-0.92; P<0.001) in patients with heart failure and mildly reduced or preserved ejection fraction.
6263 patients with NYHA class II-IV heart failure and LVEF >40% were enrolled. The median follow-up was 2.3 years.

queries.query: Based on these papers, what is the recommended first-line treatment for all types of heart failure?
expectedBehavior: Does NOT make a blanket recommendation — data is mixed
Notes RALES positive in HFrEF (severe), TOPCAT negative in HFpEF
Notes DAPA-HF positive in HFrEF, DELIVER positive in HFpEF
Acknowledges different drug classes and populations
Does NOT claim any single drug works for all heart failure
failurePatterns: Makes a blanket recommendation without caveats
Claims one drug is universally recommended

queries.query: Create a study guide covering all four trials
expectedBehavior: Study guide with Key Concepts, Main Findings, Methodology, Review Questions, Key Takeaways

qualityCriteria: Adversarial 'recommend treatment' question handled without blanket claims
TOPCAT negative result not glossed over
4-trial study guide covers all trials with correct stats
Drug class distinction (MRA vs SGLT2) maintained
EF population distinction (HFrEF vs HFpEF) maintained
No cross-contamination of statistics between trials
